response.justification: The role's hard floor is 8 years of C++ experience, but the candidate's resume lists no C++ in the skills section and shows only a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments involving C/C++ components; the rest of the career is dominated by DLang, TypeScript/Node.js, and web-stack work. Beyond the language gap, the candidate has no demonstrated cloud compute platform ownership, no formal technical leadership of infrastructure or DevOps teams, and no evidence of driving large-scale distributed systems projects at the architectural level required. Workshop facilitation and open-source project ownership show initiative but do not substitute for the platform engineering depth this role demands.
strengths: Long career with broad distributed-systems exposure, including a high-throughput micro-services role at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Demonstrated mentoring culture through recurring workshops, Selenium courses, and Refactoring Dojos, aligning with the mentorship responsibility
Hands-on infrastructure experience with Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and AWS CDK shows some operational breadth
concerns: C++ is essentially absent from the skillset — listed in neither the skills section nor any substantive role beyond a 6-month junior stint in 2000-2001, far short of the 8-year minimum requirement
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure initiative; cloud exposure is limited to tooling and deployment configuration
No formal technical lead title or documented experience mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers, making the preferred leadership qualifications unverifiable
